text: Re-clustering within top-level clusters yields 447 plausible sub-domains (84,228 theorems), suggesting the domain structure is hierarchical - but this is explicitly preliminary, not validated against Mathlib's module hierarchy.

comments.resolution: Updated inconclusive->supported by e-0025 (src/validate_subclusters.py@61a4c74): the 447 sub-clusters ARE now validated against Mathlib's own 2-level module hierarchy. Sub-splitting lifts agreement with 2-level modules from NMI 0.358 (top-only) to 0.506 (+0.148), tracks 2-level modules within each top cluster at 4.1x the permutation null (0.383 vs 0.094), and triples finer-grained purity (0.17->0.46). Caveat: purity is only moderate, so sub-domains are real-but-coarse, not a clean one-module-each refinement.
